entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to identify any business activity in their community or their business club, and show how the business owner influence customers about their products or services and increases Let groups share their responses with the rest of class. Then, relate their responses to the meaning of marketing and its importance. Case study: Provide a case study that portrays the marketing activities of an operating small business in the community. The case study should indicate the 4Ps (product, price, place/ distribution and promotion). Let students analyse the given case study and then answer questions related to the identified 4Ps of marketing
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of marketing for small businesses are clearly explained
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to identify any business activity in their community or their business club, and show how the business owner influence customers about their products or services and increases Let groups share their responses with the rest of class. Then, relate their responses to the meaning of marketing and its importance. Case study: Provide a case study that portrays the marketing activities of an operating small business in the community. The case study should indicate the 4Ps (product, price, place/ distribution and promotion). Let students analyse the given case study and then answer questions related to the identified 4Ps of marketing
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of marketing for small businesses are clearly explained
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to prepare a marketing plan using the 4Ps for business club products. Let students present their marketing plans to the rest of the class. Project activity: Task students to use the 4Ps to market their products made in their business club. From time to time, monitor the progress
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of marketing for small businesses are clearly explained
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Scenario: Prepare a scenario that portrays the meaning and importance of e-marketing. Guide students to summarise the lessons learned from the scenario and relate their discussion with the meaning and importance of e-marketing. Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss features of e-marketing which enable small businesses to operate effectively. Let students make presentations for common understanding. Relate their presentations with the features of e-marketing
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Scenario: Prepare a scenario that portrays the meaning and importance of e-marketing. Guide students to summarise the lessons learned from the scenario and relate their discussion with the meaning and importance of e-marketing. Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss features of e-marketing which enable small businesses to operate effectively. Let students make presentations for common understanding. Relate their presentations with the features of e-marketing
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the meaning of biography and social media. Let students share their findings from the discussions. Relate their presentations with the meaning of biography and social media. Guide students on how to create social media accounts. Skills lab: Using a template containing the features of a good biography, then ask students in manageable groups to discuss and design their business biography to uploaded in social media. Let students present their biographies and provide the feedback for improvements. Guide students to upload the biographies on their business club social media accounts
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Group activity: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss the meaning of biography and social media. Let students share their findings from the discussions. Relate their presentations with the meaning of biography and social media. Guide students on how to create social media accounts. Skills lab: Using a template containing the features of a good biography, then ask students in manageable groups to discuss and design their business biography to uploaded in social media. Let students present their biographies and provide the feedback for improvements. Guide students to upload the biographies on their business club social media accounts
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Project activity: Task students to create individual social media accounts and upload their individual business biography. Guest speaker: Invite a resourceful person to share on how they use e-marketing to market their business. Let students write a report on the lessons learnt from the presentation
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of business knowledge
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Project activity: Task students to create individual social media accounts and upload their individual business biography. Guest speaker: Invite a resourceful person to share on how they use e-marketing to market their business. Let students write a report on the lessons learnt from the presentation
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of e-marketing for small businesses are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: Group activity: Discuss school rules and regulations controlling club activities. Relate the findings from the discussions to the meaning and importance of regulations governing small business operations in Tanzania. Case study: Provide a case study portraying a business which does not pay tax & levies and sells illegal products. Guide students to analyse the case study and discuss the possible consequences for non-compliances and certification. Then discuss basic certifications and compliances procedures to consider when starting and running a business in Tanzania. Let students share their findings from group discussions. Summarise the students’ presentations by relating to the importance of compliance and certification and the basic certification and compliance procedures required to consider when starting or running a business in Tanzania. Guide students to discuss additional regulatory policies and government institutions and agencies that guide business operations in Tanzania. Guest speaker: Invite a resourceful person from government agencies responsible for regulating small business operations in Tanzania and share the following: (i) Government agencies relating with businesses in Tanzania (ii) roles of such agencies (iii) Business regulations (laws, policies) (iv) importance of complying with the regulations. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups to reflect on the guest speaker presentations by writing and submitting a report with recommendations on how they can comply with business regulations in their business club
entries.assessment: The basic aspects of the policy and regulatory environment governing small businesses in Tanzania are appropriately explored
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Solve social challenges using business skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of the basic skills of operating a small-scale business
entries.learning_activity: Reflection: Guide students to recall the importance of business compliance and certification. Relate their responses to types of licenses, importance of registration and certification and compliance. Field trip: Guide students to visit nearest businesses and find out types of licenses they have, importance of registration and consequences of non-compliance. Let them ask where they register their businesses (sole proprietorship) and thereafter discuss how the businesses are legally registered. Resourceful person: Invite a staff working at district/agency who is in charge of supervising businesses registration and licensing in the community. The resource person should share on: (i) Procedure for registration and licensing a business (ii) Penalties for non-compliance. Skills lab: Get the students into manageable groups and provide students with a business registration guide and assign them to create an action plan on the steps they will take to register their businesses with relevant authorities. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups to prepare the procedures of registering a business of their choice, then discuss or foresee the registration challenges they may encounter and the mitigation plan
entries.assessment: The procedures for registering small businesses in Tanzania are clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorm: Organise students in manageable groups to brainstorm on the meaning and importance of planning. Relate students’ responses to the meaning and importance of business planning. Case study: Organise students in manageable groups and provide a case study that portrays the plan for a small business from the community or business club. The case study should capture areas of business description, operating activities, market analysis and marketing plan. Let students discuss and share lessons learnt from the case study. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop a plan that shows business description, market analysis, operating activities and marketing plan. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Group activity: Provide students with templates of management, organisation, operations and financial plans. Let students discuss and reflect what they learned in the previous classes to create their own plans and share with the rest of class through presentation. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop management, organisation, operations and financial plans for their identified small businesses using relevant templates shared to them. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups or individually to create a business plan for their small business that includes business description, market analysis, marketing, management, organisation, operations and financial plans
entries.assessment: A plan for a small business identified from the opportunities in form two is appropriately developed
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

Project activity: Task students in manageable groups or individually to create a business plan for their small business that includes business description, market analysis, marketing, management, organisation, operations and financial plans
entries.assessment: A plan for a small business identified from the opportunities in form two is appropriately developed
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorm: Organise students in manageable groups to brainstorm on the meaning and importance of planning. Relate students’ responses to the meaning and importance of business planning. Case study: Organise students in manageable groups and provide a case study that portrays the plan for a small business from the community or business club. The case study should capture areas of business description, operating activities, market analysis and marketing plan. Let students discuss and share lessons learnt from the case study. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop a plan that shows business description, market analysis, operating activities and marketing plan. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Group activity: Provide students with templates of management, organisation, operations and financial plans. Let students discuss and reflect what they learned in the previous classes to create their own plans and share with the rest of class through presentation. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop management, organisation, operations and financial plans for their identified small businesses using relevant templates shared to them. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups or individually to create a business plan for their small business that includes business description, market analysis, marketing, management, organisation, operations and financial plans
entries.assessment: A plan for a small business identified from the opportunities in form two is appropriately developed
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Apply business theories into practice
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorm: Organise students in manageable groups to brainstorm on the meaning and importance of planning. Relate students’ responses to the meaning and importance of business planning. Case study: Organise students in manageable groups and provide a case study that portrays the plan for a small business from the community or business club. The case study should capture areas of business description, operating activities, market analysis and marketing plan. Let students discuss and share lessons learnt from the case study. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop a plan that shows business description, market analysis, operating activities and marketing plan. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Group activity: Provide students with templates of management, organisation, operations and financial plans. Let students discuss and reflect what they learned in the previous classes to create their own plans and share with the rest of class through presentation. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to develop management, organisation, operations and financial plans for their identified small businesses using relevant templates shared to them. Let students present their plans to receive feedback for further improvements. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups or individually to create a business plan for their small business that includes business description, market analysis, marketing, management, organisation, operations and financial plans
entries.assessment: A plan for a small business identified from the opportunities in form two is appropriately developed
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Apply business knowledge in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Demonstrate mastery of basic business ethics
entries.learning_activity: Brainstorming: Organise students in manageable groups to brainstorm on the norms and values that exist in their community. Relate their responses to the meaning and importance of ethics (norms and values) in operating businesses in Tanzania. Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and write personal values that influence business operations. Then, let them discuss and present how they will promote those values in the business club activities. Let students develop ethics guides for their businesses. Project activity: Task students in manageable groups to write a report on the impact of bad/poor personal values on the business operations and draw/develop a plan of how they can help the business owner to improve. Group activity: Guide students in manageable groups to search the business ethics advocated by Fair competition commission (FCC) and Zanzibar Fair competition commission (ZFCC), let them discuss in a class. Then, summarise and conclude the basic tenets of ethics applicable for small businesses in Tanzania
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of ethics for small businesses clearly described
entries.references: Business Studies Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
